components.name: Accordion
examples: What is Tale UI? Tale UI is a comprehensive design system and component library built with React and CSS custom properties. How do I install it? Install via npm or pnpm: pnpm add @tale-ui/react @tale-ui/react-styles

<Accordion.Root allowsMultipleExpanded>
  <Accordion.Item id="a">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section One</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>
      Multiple items can be open at the same time.
    </Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item id="b">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Section Two</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>
      Try opening this while the first section is still open.
    </Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

<Accordion.Root defaultExpandedKeys={['a']}>
  <Accordion.Item id="a">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Initially Open</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>
      This section is open by default.
    </Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
  <Accordion.Item id="b">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Initially Closed</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>
      This section starts closed.
    </Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

<Accordion.Root isDisabled>
  <Accordion.Item id="a">
    <Accordion.Header>
      <Accordion.Trigger>Disabled Item</Accordion.Trigger>
    </Accordion.Header>
    <Accordion.Panel>
      This content cannot be revealed because the accordion is disabled.
    </Accordion.Panel>
  </Accordion.Item>
</Accordion.Root>

pitfalls.summary: Accordion.Trigger must go inside Accordion.Header
Use Accordion.Panel for content, NOT Accordion.Content
pitfalls.detail: there is no `Accordion.Content` sub-part.

pitfalls.summary: No type prop
pitfalls.detail: use `allowsMultipleExpanded` on `Accordion.Root` to allow multiple panels open simultaneously.

pitfalls.summary: Uses defaultExpandedKeys (array), NOT defaultOpen, defaultValue, or defaultSelectedKey
pitfalls.detail: pass an array of item `id` strings to set the initially expanded panels.

components.name: AlertDialog
components.description: Blocking dialog for destructive or irreversible actions requiring explicit user confirmation

pitfalls.summary: No AlertDialog.Cancel or AlertDialog.Action sub-parts
pitfalls.detail: use `<Button>` inside `AlertDialog.Actions` for Cancel and Confirm.

pitfalls.summary: AlertDialog.Content is required inside AlertDialog.Popup
pitfalls.detail: it provides `role="alertdialog"`; do not place `Title` and `Description` directly inside `Popup`.

components.name: Autocomplete

pitfalls.summary: No phantom sub-parts: no Autocomplete.Label, Autocomplete.Popover, Autocomplete.Trigger, or Autocomplete.Value
No controlled state props on Autocomplete.Root
pitfalls.detail: filter state is derived from the `filter` function and the input value managed internally.

pitfalls.summary: Use <Autocomplete> for any prompt that asks for an autocomplete, inline search, or a filtered list of options
pitfalls.detail: when the request is to search/filter items such as fruits, countries, commands, or members, render `Autocomplete.Root` with `useFilter`, `Autocomplete.SearchField`, `Autocomplete.Input`, and `Autocomplete.ListBox` instead of leaving the file empty or substituting `SearchField` or `Combobox`.
antiPatterns: import { SearchField } from '@tale-ui/react/search-field'; export function FruitSearch() { return <SearchField.Root><SearchField.Input placeholder="Search fruits..." /></SearchField.Root>; }
fixes: import { Autocomplete, useFilter } from '@tale-ui/react/autocomplete'; export function FruitAutocomplete() { const { contains } = useFilter({ sensitivity: 'base' }); return <Autocomplete.Root filter={contains}><Autocomplete.SearchField aria-label="Search fruits"><Autocomplete.Input placeholder="Search fruits..." /></Autocomplete.SearchField><Autocomplete.ListBox aria-label="Fruits"><Autocomplete.Item id="apple" textValue="Apple">Apple</Autocomplete.Item></Autocomplete.ListBox></Autocomplete.Root>; }

pitfalls.summary: Avatar.Indicator must wrap OUTSIDE Avatar.Root
Avatar.Group propagates size to children
pitfalls.detail: do not set `size` on individual `Avatar.Root` inside a Group unless intentionally overriding the group size.

pitfalls.summary: size accepts 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl', '2xl'
pitfalls.detail: NOT the short forms `'s'`, `'m'`, or `'l'`.

pitfalls.summary: Avatar.CompanyIcon must wrap Avatar.Root from outside
pitfalls.detail: like `Avatar.Indicator`, the badge is absolutely positioned on the wrapper. Placing it inside `Avatar.Root` will clip it.

pitfalls.summary: Avatar.ProfilePhoto accepts Avatar.Image then Avatar.Fallback as direct children
pitfalls.detail: do not nest `Avatar.Root` inside `ProfilePhoto`; it renders its own inner avatar element.

pitfalls.summary: Avatar.Count accepts children text, not a count prop
pitfalls.detail: pass the overflow label string (e.g. `"+5"`) as children; there is no `count` prop and passing one causes a TypeScript error.

pitfalls.summary: Avatar.ProfilePhoto size does not accept 'xl' or '2xl' — valid values are 'xs', 'sm', 'md', 'lg'
pitfalls.detail: `ProfilePhotoSize` is a narrower type than `Avatar.Root` size; passing `size="xl"` or `size="2xl"` causes `Type '"xl"' is not assignable to type 'ProfilePhotoSize | undefined'`. Use `size="lg"` for the largest profile photo.

components.name: BadgeGroup

pitfalls.summary: Always use BadgeGroup.Root
pitfalls.detail: `BadgeGroup` is a namespace. Rendering `<BadgeGroup>` directly is a TypeScript error.

pitfalls.summary: addonText is the primary distinguishing feature
pitfalls.detail: omitting it renders a plain badge with no addon segment.

pitfalls.summary: No 'link' or 'secondary' variant — for Cancel or secondary-action buttons always use variant="neutral"
size accepts 'sm', 'md', 'lg' only — no 'xs'
pitfalls.detail: There is no `'xs'` size. The smallest available size is `'sm'`.

pitfalls.summary: No asChild prop
pitfalls.detail: `Button` does not support `asChild`. To render a button as a link, use the `Link` component or wrap with a router link adapter.

pitfalls.summary: No icon, leftIcon, rightIcon, or startIcon prop
pitfalls.detail: Place an `<Icon>` component (or any icon) directly as a child of `Button`. Position before or after the label text as needed.

pitfalls.summary: Button has no href prop — it renders a <button>, not a link
pitfalls.detail: passing `href` causes a TypeScript assignability error. For navigational links styled as buttons, use `Link` from `@tale-ui/react/link` with BEM class names.

components.name: Calendar
examples:
<Calendar.Root>
  <Calendar.Header>
    <Calendar.PreviousButton />
    <Calendar.Heading />
    <Calendar.NextButton />
  </Calendar.Header>
  <Calendar.Grid>
    <Calendar.GridHeader>
      {(day) => <Calendar.GridHeaderCell>{day}</Calendar.GridHeaderCell>}
    </Calendar.GridHeader>
    <Calendar.GridBody>

    </Calendar.GridBody>
  </Calendar.Grid>
</Calendar.Root>

<Calendar.Root isDisabled>
  <Calendar.Header>
    <Calendar.PreviousButton />
    <Calendar.Heading />
    <Calendar.NextButton />
  </Calendar.Header>
  <Calendar.Grid>
    <Calendar.GridHeader>
      {(day) => <Calendar.GridHeaderCell>{day}</Calendar.GridHeaderCell>}
    </Calendar.GridHeader>
    <Calendar.GridBody>
      {(date) => <Calendar.Cell date={date} />}
    </Calendar.GridBody>
  </Calendar.Grid>
</Calendar.Root>

import { today, getLocalTimeZone } from '@internationalized/date';

<Calendar.Root defaultValue={today(getLocalTimeZone())} isReadOnly>
  <Calendar.Header>
    <Calendar.PreviousButton />
    <Calendar.Heading />
    <Calendar.NextButton />
  </Calendar.Header>
  <Calendar.Grid>
    <Calendar.GridHeader>
      {(day) => <Calendar.GridHeaderCell>{day}</Calendar.GridHeaderCell>}
    </Calendar.GridHeader>
    <Calendar.GridBody>
      {(date) => <Calendar.Cell date={date} />}
    </Calendar.GridBody>
  </Calendar.Grid>
</Calendar.Root>

pitfalls.summary: Navigation buttons and heading must be inside Calendar.Header
Use Calendar.GridHeaderCell in GridHeader, not Calendar.Cell
pitfalls.detail: `GridHeader` passes day name strings to its render prop; `Cell` expects a date object and crashes with `TypeError` if used here.

pitfalls.summary: Do not add vertical margin or padding to Calendar.Heading
pitfalls.detail: it is positioned by `Calendar.Header`'s flex layout; extra margin breaks alignment.

pitfalls.summary: Use Calendar for any prompt that asks for a date calendar picker, standalone calendar, or single date selection
pitfalls.detail: when the request is to display a calendar for picking a single date, render `Calendar.Root` with the full header and grid structure instead of leaving the file empty or substituting `DatePicker`.
fixes: import { Calendar } from '@tale-ui/react/calendar'; export function DateCalendarPicker() { return <Calendar.Root><Calendar.Header><Calendar.PreviousButton /><Calendar.Heading /><Calendar.NextButton /></Calendar.Header><Calendar.Grid><Calendar.GridHeader>{(day) => <Calendar.GridHeaderCell>{day}</Calendar.GridHeaderCell>}</Calendar.GridHeader><Calendar.GridBody>{(date) => <Calendar.Cell date={date} />}</Calendar.GridBody></Calendar.Grid></Calendar.Root>; }

examples:

pitfalls.summary: Import ColorSwatch separately from @tale-ui/react/color-swatch
Uses value/onChange, not onSelectionChange
pitfalls.detail: The controlled API uses `value` and `onChange`. There is no `onSelectionChange` prop.

pitfalls.summary: value does NOT accept null or undefined
pitfalls.detail: `ColorSwatchPicker.Root` requires a non-nullable color value when controlled. Use `defaultValue` for uncontrolled usage without an initial value.

pitfalls.summary: No ColorSwatchPicker.List
pitfalls.detail: There is no `List` sub-part. Place `ColorSwatchPicker.Item` elements directly inside `ColorSwatchPicker.Root`.

pitfalls.summary: value/onChange use Color objects, not plain strings — initialize state with parseColor()
pitfalls.detail: `onChange` receives a `Color` object; passing a `Dispatch<SetStateAction<string>>` setter directly causes `Type 'Dispatch<SetStateAction<string>>' is not assignable to type '(value: Color) => void'`. Initialize state with `parseColor()` so the inferred type matches. Import `parseColor` from `@tale-ui/react/color-area`. To display the selected color as a string, call `.toString('css')` on the Color value.

pitfalls.summary: Use <ColorSwatchPicker.Root> for any prompt that asks for selectable color swatches, a color palette, or a swatch picker
pitfalls.detail: when the request is to choose from a visible set of color options, render `ColorSwatchPicker.Root` with direct `ColorSwatchPicker.Item` children and a nested `ColorSwatch` for each option instead of leaving the file empty or substituting `ColorSwatch`, `ColorPicker`, or custom buttons.
antiPatterns: import { ColorSwatch } from '@tale-ui/react/color-swatch'; export function Palette() { return <ColorSwatch color="#3b82f6" />; }
fixes: import { ColorSwatchPicker } from '@tale-ui/react/color-swatch-picker'; import { ColorSwatch } from '@tale-ui/react/color-swatch'; export function Palette() { return <ColorSwatchPicker.Root defaultValue="#3b82f6"><ColorSwatchPicker.Item color="#3b82f6"><ColorSwatch /></ColorSwatchPicker.Item></ColorSwatchPicker.Root>; }

pitfalls.summary: Drawer.Root uses open/onOpenChange, not isOpen
Drawer.Backdrop must be a self-closing sibling of Drawer.Popup, not a wrapper

pitfalls.summary: No layout sub-parts (Drawer.Actions, Drawer.Body, Drawer.Header, Drawer.Footer)
pitfalls.detail: use plain `<div>` elements with flex layout for content sections.

pitfalls.summary: No placement prop on Drawer.Root or Drawer.Popup — use only layout inline styles on Drawer.Popup
pitfalls.detail: placement is controlled via CSS, but keep `Drawer.Popup` inline styles limited to layout-only properties such as positioning and sizing; move visual styles like `background`, `boxSizing`, `border`, and `boxShadow` to Tale UI defaults, variants, or wrapper CSS.

pitfalls.summary: Use <Drawer> for any prompt that asks for a drawer, side drawer, side sheet, or slide-over panel
pitfalls.detail: when the request is for an overlay panel that slides in from a screen edge, render `Drawer.Root` with `Drawer.Backdrop`, `Drawer.Popup`, `Drawer.Title`, and a `Drawer.Close` button instead of leaving the file empty or substituting `Dialog`. Use CSS on `Drawer.Popup` to position it on the right or left because `Drawer` has no placement prop.
antiPatterns: import { Dialog } from '@tale-ui/react/dialog'; export function SettingsDrawer() { return <Dialog.Root />; }
fixes: import * as React from 'react'; import { Drawer } from '@tale-ui/react/drawer'; export function SettingsDrawer() { const [open, setOpen] = React.useState(true); return <Drawer.Root open={open} onOpenChange={setOpen}><Drawer.Backdrop /><Drawer.Popup style={{ marginLeft: 'auto' }}><Drawer.Title>Settings</Drawer.Title><Drawer.Close className="tale-button tale-button--neutral tale-button--md">Close</Drawer.Close></Drawer.Popup></Drawer.Root>; }

pitfalls.summary: Use FileUpload for any prompt that asks for a file upload area, dropzone uploader, or uploaded-file list with progress

props.name: icon
props.description: A lucide-react icon component (e.g., ChevronDown).

props.name: size
props.description: Size variant. Defaults to 'md'.

props.name: label
props.description: Accessible label. If omitted, icon is decorative (aria-hidden).

examples:
import { AlertCircle } from 'lucide-react';

<Icon icon={AlertCircle} label="Warning" />

pitfalls.summary: icon prop takes a component reference, not an instance
Import lucide icons from lucide-react root, not deep subpaths
pitfalls.detail: deep imports like `lucide-react/dist/esm/icons/bell` are not part of the public API and break on package updates.

pitfalls.summary: Avoid Icon Image name collisions
pitfalls.detail: `Icon` expects an SVG icon component, not the `Image` component from `@tale-ui/react/image`. When a file-tree or favicon row needs an image glyph, import a lucide icon with a non-conflicting name such as `ImageIcon` or `FileImage`.
antiPatterns: import { Image } from 'lucide-react'; import { Image } from '@tale-ui/react/image';

pitfalls.summary: size accepts 'sm', 'md', 'lg', 'xl' only
pitfalls.detail: NOT the token-style `'s'`/`'m'`/`'l'` suffixes used in CSS tokens.

components.name: IconButton

props.name: disabled
props.description: Alias for `isDisabled` for convenience.

props.name: pending
props.description: Alias for `isPending` for convenience.

examples:
import { Plus, Settings, Search, Trash2, Download } from 'lucide-react';

<IconButton variant="primary" aria-label="Add"><Icon icon={Plus} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="neutral" aria-label="Settings"><Icon icon={Settings} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="ghost" aria-label="Search"><Icon icon={Search} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="danger" aria-label="Delete"><Icon icon={Trash2} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="inverse" aria-label="Download"><Icon icon={Download} /></IconButton>

<IconButton variant="neutral" size="sm" aria-label="Small"><Icon icon={Heart} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="neutral" size="md" aria-label="Medium"><Icon icon={Heart} /></IconButton>
<IconButton variant="neutral" size="lg" aria-label="Large"><Icon icon={Heart} /></IconButton>

<IconButton variant="ghost" isDisabled aria-label="Search">
  <Icon icon={Search} />
</IconButton>

<IconButton variant="ghost" isPending aria-label="Search">
  <Icon icon={Search} />
</IconButton>

pitfalls.summary: aria-label is required
Do not nest <IconButton> inside a trigger component
pitfalls.detail: triggers render their own `<button>` element; nesting an `IconButton` creates a `<button>` inside a `<button>`, which is invalid HTML.

pitfalls.summary: Use <IconButton> for icon-only actions, not <Button> or a native <button>
pitfalls.detail: when a prompt asks for an "icon button", render the `IconButton` component itself and include an `<Icon>` child for the glyph. Do not substitute `Button` with an icon, and do not omit the `Icon` child.
antiPatterns: import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; import { Trash } from 'lucide-react'; export function DeleteButton() { return <Button variant="danger" aria-label="Delete item"><Trash /></Button>; }
fixes: import { IconButton } from '@tale-ui/react/icon-button'; import { Icon } from '@tale-ui/react/icon'; import { Trash } from 'lucide-react'; export function DeleteButton() { return <IconButton variant="danger" aria-label="Delete item"><Icon icon={Trash} /></IconButton>; }

components.name: ImageCropper
components.description: Interactive image crop region with aspect ratio and circular crop support
examples:
<ImageCropper.Root crop={crop} onChange={setCrop} aspect={16 / 9}>
  <ImageCropper.Img src="/landscape.jpg" alt="Crop" />
</ImageCropper.Root>

<ImageCropper.Root crop={crop} onChange={setCrop} aspect={1} circularCrop>
  <ImageCropper.Img src="/photo.jpg" alt="Crop avatar" />
</ImageCropper.Root>

pitfalls.summary: Never apply non-layout inline styles (objectFit, objectPosition) to ImageCropper.Img — use a wrapper element for those CSS overrides instead
pitfalls.detail: `ImageCropper.Img` only accepts layout styles (`maxHeight`, `width`) directly on its `style` prop; passing visual rendering styles like `objectFit` causes a validation error.

pitfalls.summary: Use <ImageCropper> for any prompt that asks for image cropping, avatar photo cropping, or a fixed-aspect crop area
pitfalls.detail: when the request is to crop a profile photo, avatar, or other image, render `ImageCropper.Root` with an `ImageCropper.Img` child instead of leaving the file empty or substituting `Image`. For square avatar crops, set `aspect={1}`.
antiPatterns: import { Image } from '@tale-ui/react/image'; export function AvatarCropper() { return <Image src="/avatar.jpg" alt="User avatar" />; }
fixes: import * as React from 'react'; import { ImageCropper, centerCrop, makeAspectCrop } from '@tale-ui/react/image-cropper'; import type { Crop } from '@tale-ui/react/image-cropper'; function centerAspectCrop(mediaWidth: number, mediaHeight: number, aspect: number) { return centerCrop(makeAspectCrop({ unit: '%', width: 80 }, aspect, mediaWidth, mediaHeight), mediaWidth, mediaHeight); } export function AvatarCropper() { const [crop, setCrop] = React.useState<Crop>(); function handleImageLoad(event: React.SyntheticEvent<HTMLImageElement>) { const { width, height } = event.currentTarget; setCrop(centerAspectCrop(width, height, 1)); } return <ImageCropper.Root crop={crop} onChange={setCrop} aspect={1}><ImageCropper.Img src="/avatar.jpg" alt="User avatar to crop" onLoad={handleImageLoad} style={{ maxHeight: 320, width: '100%' }} /></ImageCropper.Root>; }

pitfalls.summary: Place Input.Label inside Input.Root
pitfalls.detail: the label is semantically associated with the input via React Aria's `TextField`, so it must remain inside `Input.Root`, not outside the `InputGroup.Root`.

pitfalls.summary: Buttons inside InputGroup.Addon may conflict with addon styles
pitfalls.detail: Tale UI buttons carry their own border-radius and box-shadow that can conflict with the addon's flush layout. Adjust via `className` overrides or use a plain `<button>`.

pitfalls.summary: Use a single Input.Label inside Input.Root
pitfalls.detail: `InputGroup.Root` keeps the label associated with the input while aligning the addons to the input control row.

components.name: InputTags
components.description: Tag chip input — type to add tags, press Enter to commit, Backspace to remove

props.name: tagPlacement
props.description: Where to render the tag chips — inside the input area (`'inline'`) or below it in a separate row (`'below'`). @default 'inline'

props.name: size
props.description: Size variant. @default 'md'

props.name: placeholder
props.description: Placeholder text for the text input.

props.name: isDisabled
props.description: Whether the field is disabled.

props.name: isInvalid
props.description: Whether the field is in an error state.

props.name: isRequired
props.description: Whether the field is required.

props.name: value
props.description: Controlled value — array of tag strings.

props.name: defaultValue
props.description: Initial tags for uncontrolled mode.

props.name: onChange
props.description: Called whenever the tag list changes.

props.name: onTagAdded
props.description: Called when a tag is successfully added.

props.name: onTagRemoved
props.description: Called when a tag is removed.

props.name: allowDuplicates
props.description: Allow duplicate tag values. @default false

props.name: maxTags
props.description: Maximum number of tags allowed.

props.name: validate
props.description: Validation function — return `true` to accept a new tag, `false` to reject it.

props.name: label
props.description: Label text displayed above the field.

props.name: description
props.description: Helper text displayed below the field.

props.name: errorMessage
props.description: Error message shown when `isInvalid` is true.

examples: import { InputTags } from '@tale-ui/react/input-tags';

<InputTags.Root
  label="Technologies"
  placeholder="Add a technology…"
  tagPlacement="inline"
  defaultValue={['React', 'TypeScript']}
  onChange={setTags}
/>

<InputTags.Root
  label="Tags"
  placeholder="Add a tag…"
  tagPlacement="below"
  defaultValue={['design', 'ui']}
  onChange={setTags}
/>

<InputTags.Root size="sm" label="Small" placeholder="Add tag…" />
<InputTags.Root size="md" label="Medium" placeholder="Add tag…" />
<InputTags.Root size="lg" label="Large" placeholder="Add tag…" />

{/* Invalid with error message */}
<InputTags.Root
  label="Required skills"
  isInvalid
  errorMessage="At least one skill is required."
  isRequired
/>

{/* Disabled */}
<InputTags.Root
  label="Skills"
  isDisabled
  defaultValue={['React', 'TypeScript']}
/>

<InputTags.Root
  label="Hashtags"
  placeholder="#tag"
  validate={(value) => value.startsWith('#') && value.length > 1}
  description="Each tag must start with #"
/>

<InputTags.Root
  label="Up to 3 tags"
  maxTags={3}
  description="Maximum 3 tags allowed."
/>

pitfalls.summary: Backspace/ArrowLeft keyboard navigation applies to inline mode only
pitfalls.detail: in `tagPlacement="below"` mode, pressing Backspace when the input is empty does not focus the last tag.

pitfalls.summary: InputTags does not integrate with React Aria form contexts
pitfalls.detail: it renders a plain `<div>` container. For form validation, use the `isInvalid`/`errorMessage` props directly rather than wrapping in a `Field`.

pitfalls.summary: In controlled mode, pass a stable array reference when the value hasn't changed
pitfalls.detail: the ID reconciliation logic uses reference equality (`===`) to skip re-reconciliation. Creating a new array on every render forces re-reconciliation on every render (though it remains functionally correct).

pitfalls.summary: Use InputTags.Root for any prompt that asks for a tag-entry field, skills input, chip input, or add-tags UI
pitfalls.detail: when the request is specifically to let users enter multiple tags such as skills, render `InputTags.Root` directly with `label`, `placeholder`, `description`, and `maxTags` as needed instead of leaving the file empty or substituting a different field component.
fixes: import { InputTags } from '@tale-ui/react/input-tags'; export function SkillsField() { return <InputTags.Root label="Skills" placeholder="Add a skill..." description="Enter up to 5 skills. Press Enter to add each one." maxTags={5} />; }

pitfalls.summary: Image size should ideally be 750 × 1624 px
pitfalls.detail: the SVG pattern is sized to this resolution. Other resolutions will be stretched or compressed.

pitfalls.summary: The exported name is IPhoneMockup (capital P), not IphoneMockup
pitfalls.detail: the import destructure and JSX tag must use `IPhoneMockup`; `IphoneMockup` (lowercase 'p') does not exist as an export and causes a missing-component error.

pitfalls.summary: Menu.MenuList is required between Menu.Popover and Menu.Item
Menu.Separator goes inside Menu.MenuList, not as a sibling
pitfalls.detail: a separator outside the list renders outside `role="menu"`.

pitfalls.summary: Menu.Root does not accept onAction or open
pitfalls.detail: pass `onAction` to `Menu.Item`; open state is managed internally.

pitfalls.summary: Use <Menu> for any prompt that asks for a dropdown menu, action menu, or options menu
pitfalls.detail: when the request is for a button-triggered list of actions, render the `Menu` component itself instead of a plain `Button`, custom popover markup, or an empty placeholder.
antiPatterns: import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; export function FileActions() { return <Button>File</Button>; }
fixes: import { Menu } from '@tale-ui/react/menu'; export function FileActions() { return <Menu.Root><Menu.Trigger className="tale-button tale-button--neutral tale-button--md">File</Menu.Trigger><Menu.Popover><Menu.MenuList><Menu.Item id="new">New</Menu.Item><Menu.Separator /><Menu.Item id="close">Close</Menu.Item></Menu.MenuList></Menu.Popover></Menu.Root>; }

pitfalls.summary: textValue is required on items when search is enabled
pitfalls.detail: the React Aria `Autocomplete` component uses `textValue` to filter items. Without it, the search won't filter items correctly.

pitfalls.summary: Never import Selection from react-aria-components for MultiSelect state — derive the type from the component's props
pitfalls.detail: `react-aria-components` is not available in consumer projects; importing from it causes "Cannot find module" TypeScript errors. Derive the selection type from `MultiSelect.Root` props instead.

pitfalls.summary: The popover width matches the trigger width at open time
pitfalls.detail: it is captured on trigger click using `getBoundingClientRect()`. If the trigger resizes after the popover opens, the widths may diverge.

pitfalls.summary: provider prop is required
Use SocialButton for any social sign-in, sign-up, or OAuth button prompt
pitfalls.detail: when a prompt asks for a Google, GitHub, Apple, X, or Facebook auth button, render the `SocialButton` component itself instead of a generic `Button`, custom markup, or an empty placeholder. This ensures the correct built-in provider styling and icon are used.
antiPatterns: import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; export function SignInWithGoogle() { return <Button>Sign in with Google</Button>; }
fixes: import { SocialButton } from '@tale-ui/react/social-button'; export function SignInWithGoogle() { return <SocialButton provider="google">Sign in with Google</SocialButton>; }

pitfalls.summary: size only accepts 'sm' or 'md' — 'lg' does not exist
pitfalls.detail: passing `size="lg"` causes `Type '"lg"' is not assignable to type 'Size | undefined'`. When the prompt does not request a specific size, omit the prop and use the default.

<Text as="label" variant="label" size="s">Form label</Text>

<Text as="h1" variant="display" size="l" color="accent">
  Hero Title
</Text>
<Text as="p" variant="text" size="m" color="muted">
  A supporting description rendered as a paragraph.
</Text>
<Text variant="mono" size="s" color="muted">
  const x = 42;
</Text>

pitfalls.summary:
Never use bare

paragraph tags or for visible text
antiPatterns: This action cannot be undone.

pitfalls.summary: size only accepts 'xs', 's', 'm', 'l'
pitfalls.detail: no other size tokens are valid.

pitfalls.summary: Monospace variant is "mono", NOT "code"
pitfalls.detail: use `variant="mono"` for monospace text.

pitfalls.summary: variant+size compound strings like 'heading-m' or 'body-m' are not valid — always pass them as two separate props
pitfalls.detail: compound strings fail TypeScript; there is also no `'body'` variant, use `variant="text"` for body copy. When generating heading+body content-section pairs, also use `color="muted"` (not `color="secondary"`) for supporting text.

pitfalls.summary: No htmlFor prop on Text
pitfalls.detail: use `as="label"` and pass `htmlFor` as a standard HTML attribute.

pitfalls.summary: as prop only accepts 'p', 'span', 'div', 'h1'–'h6', 'label'
pitfalls.detail: not `'strong'`, `'code'`, `'pre'`, `'kbd'`, or other elements. For keyboard shortcut markup use a native `<kbd>` HTML element directly.

pitfalls.summary: color only accepts 'default', 'muted', 'accent'
pitfalls.detail: no other color values are valid.

pitfalls.summary: Text has no boolean muted prop — use color="muted" for secondary text, plus separate variant/size props when needed
pitfalls.detail: `muted` is a common shorthand in other libraries, but Tale UI `Text` controls tone only through the `color` prop; using `<Text muted>` causes a TypeScript error.
antiPatterns: <Text muted variant="body-sm">This action cannot be undone.</Text>
fixes: <Text as="p" color="muted">This action cannot be undone.</Text>
<Text as="p" variant="text" size="s" color="muted">This action cannot be undone.</Text>

pitfalls.summary: Use color="muted" for muted secondary paragraph copy
pitfalls.detail: prompts that ask for subdued body text should not be translated into generic component tokens like `'md'` or neutral color names; on `Text`, the muted tone is `color="muted"` and paragraph sizing should usually stay implicit unless the prompt asks for a specific text scale.
antiPatterns: <Text size="md" color="neutral">This action cannot be undone.</Text>
fixes: <Text as="p" color="muted">This action cannot be undone.</Text>

pitfalls.summary: color only accepts 'default', 'muted', 'accent'
pitfalls.detail: no other color values are valid. Common wrong values include 'secondary', 'neutral', 'gray', 'dim', and status tokens like 'success', 'error', 'warning' (which are valid on DotIcon/Badge but not on Text); all map to 'muted'.

pitfalls.summary: No 'link', 'code', or 'caption' variant
pitfalls.detail: valid values are `'display'`, `'heading'`, `'title'`, `'label'`, `'text'`, `'mono'`.

pitfalls.summary: Never apply non-layout inline styles (fontWeight, fontSize, textDecoration, fontStyle) to Text
pitfalls.detail: use `variant` and `size` props for typography control; for bold labels inside buttons or icon-only toggles, use a plain text node rather than a `<Text>` wrapper with `style={{ fontWeight: ... }}`.

pitfalls.summary: Text has no weight prop — use variant="label" for label-weight text
pitfalls.detail: there is no `weight` prop on `Text`; passing `weight="medium"` or any weight string causes `Type '{ weight: string; ... }' is not assignable to type 'TextProps'`. To render bold or label-weight text use `variant="label"`.

components.description: Rich-text editor built on Tiptap v3 with simple and advanced toolbars
examples:

pitfalls.summary: TextEditor.Root does not accept label or maxLength props
pitfalls.detail: `label` and `maxLength` are not valid on `TextEditor.Root`; use `TextEditor.Label` for the visible label and place `maxLength` on `TextEditor.Content`.

examples:

pitfalls.summary: TextField.Root accepts name — HTML input attributes (type, minLength, maxLength, placeholder) go on TextField.Input.
There is NO PasswordInput component
pitfalls.detail: for password fields, use `TextField` with `type="password"` on `TextField.Input`.

components.name: TimeField
examples:
<TimeField.Root>
  <TimeField.DateInput style={{ display: 'flex' }}>
    {(segment) => <TimeField.Segment segment={segment} />}
  </TimeField.DateInput>
</TimeField.Root>

  </TimeField.DateInput>

pitfalls.summary: No extra sub-parts: TimeField.Group, TimeField.Trigger, TimeField.Input, TimeField.Popover, TimeField.Dialog do not exist
The input sub-part is TimeField.DateInput, not TimeField.Input
pitfalls.detail: There is no `TimeField.Input`. The correct sub-part for the segmented time input is `TimeField.DateInput`.

pitfalls.summary: Property is hour (singular), not hours
pitfalls.detail: React Aria uses `hour` (singular) for the hour segment value. Using `hours` will be silently ignored.

components.name: ToggleButton
examples: <ToggleButton isDisabled>Disabled</ToggleButton>

<ToggleButtonGroup aria-label="Text formatting">
  <ToggleButton>Bold</ToggleButton>
  <ToggleButton>Italic</ToggleButton>
  <ToggleButton>Underline</ToggleButton>
</ToggleButtonGroup>

<ToggleButton size="sm">Small</ToggleButton>
<ToggleButton size="md">Medium</ToggleButton>
<ToggleButton size="lg">Large</ToggleButton>

pitfalls.summary: Uses isSelected/defaultSelected for toggle state, not pressed
Does NOT accept a value prop.
pitfalls.detail: anti-pattern: `<ToggleButton value="bold">Bold</ToggleButton>`

pitfalls.summary: Use <ToggleButton> for any prompt that asks for a toggle button or formatting toggle
pitfalls.detail: when the request is for a toggleable action such as bold, italic, underline, mute, favorite, or similar on/off formatting/state controls, render the `ToggleButton` component itself instead of a generic `Button`, `Toolbar.Button`, custom button markup, or an empty placeholder.
antiPatterns: import { Button } from '@tale-ui/react/button'; export function BoldToggle() { return <Button>Bold</Button>; }
fixes: import { ToggleButton } from '@tale-ui/react/toggle-button'; export function BoldToggle() { return <ToggleButton>Bold</ToggleButton>; }

components.name: ToggleButtonGroup
examples:
import { ToggleButtonGroup } from '@tale-ui/react/toggle-group';
import { ToggleButton } from '@tale-ui/react/toggle-button';

<ToggleButtonGroup aria-label="Text formatting">
  <ToggleButton>Bold</ToggleButton>
  <ToggleButton>Italic</ToggleButton>
  <ToggleButton>Underline</ToggleButton>
</ToggleButtonGroup>

pitfalls.summary: No Radix-style type, value, or onValueChange props
Requires aria-label or aria-labelledby
pitfalls.detail: React Aria logs a console warning at runtime if neither is provided.

pitfalls.summary: Use <ToggleButtonGroup> for any prompt that asks for a toggle button group, segmented alignment control, or mutually exclusive option set
pitfalls.detail: when the request is for grouped toggles such as text alignment (Left / Center / Right), view mode switchers, or any set of options where one or more can be selected, render `ToggleButtonGroup` (from `@tale-ui/react/toggle-group`) wrapping individual `ToggleButton` children instead of leaving the file empty or substituting a different component. Always pass `aria-label` and `selectionMode`.
fixes: import { ToggleButtonGroup } from '@tale-ui/react/toggle-group'; import { ToggleButton } from '@tale-ui/react/toggle-button'; export function TextAlignmentGroup() { return <ToggleButtonGroup aria-label="Text alignment" selectionMode="single"><ToggleButton>Left</ToggleButton><ToggleButton>Center</ToggleButton><ToggleButton>Right</ToggleButton></ToggleButtonGroup>; }

No standalone ToolbarButton named export

pitfalls.summary: For toggleable toolbar buttons, use <ToggleButton> from @tale-ui/react/toggle-button
pitfalls.detail: Place a `ToggleButton` inside the toolbar instead of `Toolbar.Button` when toggling state is needed.

components.name: VideoPlayer
components.description: Native video player with custom controls, keyboard shortcuts, and trickplay

props.name: src
props.description: URL of the video to play.

props.name: type
props.description: MIME type of the video. @default 'video/mp4'

props.name: autoPlay
props.description: Whether the video should auto-play on load.

props.name: thumbnailUrl
props.description: URL of a thumbnail image shown before playback starts.

props.name: thumbnailAlt
props.description: Alt text for the thumbnail image.

props.name: showThumbnailOverlay
props.description: Show a semi-transparent overlay over the thumbnail.

props.name: size
props.description: Size of the video player. - `'sm'` — compact; hides progress bar - `'md'` — standard controls - `'lg'` — full controls including volume slider and playback speed @default 'md'

examples:
import { VideoPlayer } from '@tale-ui/react/video-player';

<VideoPlayer.Root src="/intro.mp4" size="md" />

<VideoPlayer.Root
  src="/demo.mp4"
  thumbnailUrl="/thumb.jpg"
  thumbnailAlt="Product demo"
  size="lg"
/>

pitfalls.summary: VideoPlayer.Root does NOT accept a style prop — wrap it in a layout element to control width or maxWidth
pitfalls.detail: `VideoPlayer.Root` only supports its documented component props plus `className`; passing inline layout styles directly to it causes a TypeScript error because `style` is not part of `VideoPlayerProps`.
antiPatterns: <VideoPlayer.Root src="/demo.mp4" size="md" thumbnailUrl="/thumb.jpg" thumbnailAlt="Product demo" style={{ width: '64rem', maxWidth: '100%' }} />
